violations.text: Several Food items in the Open Air Cooler were observed held at 43 to 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were removed from refrigeration and voluntarily disposed of by the PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

The Open Air Cooler in the retail area was not in good repair. The unit was observed to be operating holding an ambient temperature of 40 to 47 degrees Fahrenheit from bottom to top shelf, with food temperatures observed at 41 to 52 degrees Fahrenheit from bottom to top shelf. Per the PIC, service is to be called in for the cooler and foods will only be maintained on the bottom shelves until fixed or repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024
